title: Identify Row with Most 1

content.problemStatement:
You are given a `2D` matrix of size n×mn×mn×m consisting only of 0′s0's0′s and 1′s1's1′s. Your task is to determine the index of the row that contains the maximum number of 1′s1's1′s. In the case of multiple rows having the same maximum count of 1′s1's1′s, return the index of the first such row.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains two integers nnn and mmm, representing the number of rows and columns in the matrix,
respectively.
The next nnn lines each contain mmm integers (000 or 111), representing the elements of the matrix.




Output Format

For each test case, output a single integer, the index (000-based) of the row that has the maximum number of 1′s1's1′s. If all rows contain only 0′s0's0′s, output −1-1−1.
constraints: Each matrix element is either 0 or 1
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with two integers n and m — the number of rows and columns of the matrix. Then follow n lines, each containing m space‑separated integers (0 or 1) representing the matrix rows.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line: the 0‑based index of the row that contains the maximum number of 1's. If every row contains only 0's, output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: Row 1 has two 1's, the most.
Test case 2: Row 3 has four 1's, the most.
Test case 3: No 1's in any row, so output -1.
Row 0 contains two 1's, row 1 contains one. The maximum is at index 0.
All rows consist solely of 0's, therefore the answer is -1.

solution.approach: For each row we simply count how many 1's it contains. While scanning the rows we keep the best count seen so far and the corresponding row index. If a row has a strictly larger count we update both values. Because we only need the first row in case of ties we do not update when the count is equal. This requires a single pass over the matrix, i.e. O(n·m) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n·m) per test case, O(total elements) overall

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int n=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int m=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int maxCount=0;int maxIdx=-1;for(int i=0;i<n;i++){st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int cnt=0;for(int j=0;j<m;j++){if(Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken())==1)cnt++;}if(cnt>maxCount){maxCount=cnt;maxIdx=i;}}sb.append(maxIdx).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
